MINI MINUTES:
Appearances
Timothy Baka, (415-235-8297, Plaintiff in Pro Per.
John Girarde, Esq., (415) 788-1900, jgirarde@mpbf.com, MURPHY, PEARSON, BRADLEY & FEENEY for Defendant CROWE LLP SUPERIOR. Via ZOOM

Defendant's counsel contests the Court's tentative ruling.

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF TIMOTHY BAKA'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint.

Plaintiff Timothy Baka's motion to file a first amended complaint is granted. California has "a strong policy in favor of liberal allowance of amendments;" indeed, "it is a rare case in which a court will be justified in refusing a party leave to amend his pleadings so that he may properly present his case." (Mesler v. Bragg Mgmt. Co. (1985) 39 Cal.3d 290, 296; Morgan v. Sup. Ct. (1959) Cal.App.2d 527, 530.)

Defendant argues prejudice from a summary judgment motion it has pending. However, the summary judgment opinions defendant cites feature facts plaintiffs knew of years before seeking to amend pleadings. Here, Baka identifies facts he has learned in just the past few months. (Memo. 3:23-5:18.) Defendant also argues that a trial continuance may be needed, but Baka filed his complaint less than a year ago and, given the Covid-19 pandemic, most civil cases being tried in our court are much older.

Order is signed.

Defendant Crowe Llps Special Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Complaint Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 425.16
Off Calendar Mar-05-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Plaintiff TIMOTHY BAKA (Tel: 415-235-8297) in pro per;
Atty. JOHN P. GIRARDE, Esq. of Murphy, Pearson, Bradley & Feeney (Tel: 415-788-1900) for defendant Crowe LLP.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CROWE, LLP's Special Motion To Strike Complaint Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 425.16.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Crowe, LLP's "special motion to strike complaint pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Section 425.16" is denied.

Timothy Baka is a former CFO of GCH, Inc. In 2019 Crowe, a CPA firm, audited GCH financial statements. Footnote 11 in Crowe's audit report discussed Baka's termination, without naming him. Baka nonetheless took umbrage and sued Crowe for defamation.

In the first prong of its CCP Section 425.16 analysis, Crowe says it must show that Baka's claim arises from Crowe's "free speech in connection with a public issue." (Memo. 8:28-9:26; Equilon Enterprises, LLC v. Consumer Cause, Inc. (2002) 29 Cal.4th 53, 61; CCP Section 425.16(e).) However, Baka is not "in the public eye," Crowe's footnote did not "affect large numbers of people" and no "ongoing controversy, dispute or discussion" was implicated. (FilmOn.com Inc. v. DoubleVerify Inc. (2019) 7 Cal.5th 133, 145.) Moreover, Crowe cites no case holding that a financial statement footnote has ever satisfied the first Section 425.16 prong. Because Crowe failed to establish the first prong, the second need not be reached.

Order signed.
